title: COSPLAY Asukas HELMET
wikitext:
[]: [COSPLAY] Asuka's HELMET             Quote Post by EyepatchAsuka » Fri Mar 29, 2013 8:44 pm I plan on making the helmet that Asuka wears in the beginning of EVANGELION 3.33. This one http://www.tomopop.com/ul/_tomopop/26131-medicom-proves-you-can-redo-a-rah-asuka/04-noscale.jpg http://media.mightyape.net.nz/images/products/20929811/Evangelion-3-0-16-Scale-12-RAH-Action-Figure-Asuka-Langley-Version-Q-14349379-4.jpeg I have been looking and the red helmet from halo reach was ok but after searching a little more i came across the EVA helmet from HALO 4/REACH which looks VERY similar. If someone can guide me? or tell me? or direct me to people? or how to make one please tell me!!! BTW Doing her cosplay have everything(+ eyepatch on the way) but the helmet!!!!!! HELP!!!

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Fri Mar 29, 2013 9:14 pm I remembered how before 3.0, I sent a letter to Anno saying I would give him all my money if he made Asuka "Nyan" in 3.0. Heh, he could have made 10 bucks....

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Mar 30, 2013 12:58 am If you want to make one yourself and this is your first prop, you've picked a fairly difficult project to start with. It's definitely doable but the final work will depend on how much resources are available to you (time, money, available workspace, perseverance, etc). I highly recommend going over to the replica prop forum for research/inspiration: http://www.therpf.com/ Also youtube is basically a goldmine of tutorials on anything and everything.

[]:             Quote Post by deeRez » Mon Apr 01, 2013 7:27 pm You should papier-mache it. It is a very powerful technique if done right, and the tool cost to get started is almost nonexistent.

[]:             Quote Post by Ninkie » Mon Apr 01, 2013 9:59 pm Fiberglass and bondo! Fiberglassing is just like paper mache... only toxic =D

[]:             Quote Post by deeRez » Mon Apr 01, 2013 10:42 pm Fiberglass looks interesting. If you need better captures of the helmet feel free to ask. Carry onward noble steed!

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu May 30, 2013 12:23 am Time for EDIT BUMP ---------------------OLD POST--------------------- Fiberglass you only use for reinforcing things that actually need it. For this resin alone would probably be enough. Slush casting plain urethane helmets is pretty common because it works very well. Going from the 6min aired on TV there's 3 main parts: clear visor + front frame for it + back shell. The way I'd go about it is: Sculpt the front frame and back shell out of prefered material. Glove mold with brush-on silicone. Slush cast with urethane resin. Sculpt the visor mold for vacuum forming (you can build a cheap but effecting vacuum former yourself easily for less than $100, assuming you have a decent vacuum cleaner that's not included in the cost) Vacuum form it out of prefered clear thermoplastic (iirc PETG is the most common one) Trim, prep, paint, wear, enjoy. ---------------------NEW POST--------------------- Oh boy oh boy oh boy Someone actually made one of these and showed up at Fanime SPOILER: Show
